data.summary: LangChain and LlamaIndex are two prominent frameworks for building AI applications with large language models (LLMs), particularly for implementing retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) systems. LangChain is characterized as a versatile, modular platform supporting numerous use cases, while LlamaIndex focuses more specifically on indexing and information retrieval from text-based sources. Both frameworks aim to enhance LLM capabilities by providing domain-specific knowledge and improving the accu
gaps: Detailed performance comparisons between LangChain and LlamaIndex for specific tasks
User experience and learning curve for each framework
Integration capabilities with different LLMs and data sources
Long-term maintenance and community support for each framework
